name: Diana Grass
bio: Diana completed her B.A. in Neuroscience and Behavior at Rutgers University in 2022 and immediately joined the Harvard-MIT Health Sciences and Technology Medical Engineering and Medical Physics PhD program. Previously, she conducted research on Alzheimer's disease and Autism Spectrum Disorders at the intersection of neuroimaging, neuroimmunology, and artificial intelligence. Diana's current work centers on the development of soft, stretchable bioelectronic devices designed to interface with neuroimmune systems, with the goal of enabling next-generation, targeted therapies for inflammatory diseases. Outside the lab, she enjoys traveling, cooking, photography, and spending time with her family, often seeking new experiences and adventures.
